bugs.title: companion mode tool called on info query 2
bugs.description: AI called create_itinerary_items on message 2 ('I'm near Praça do Comércio — what's a good lunch spot around here?'). The server-side guardrail blocked the call and returned status=not_needed, so no items were actually created. However, the AI should not have attempted the tool call on an informational query. The prompt discipline failed but the backend safety net caught it.
bugs.expected: No toolCall for create_itinerary_items on an informational lunch spot query
bugs.actual: toolCall with toolName=create_itinerary_items was emitted in the stream, blocked by server-side guardrail

bugs.title: companion mode tool called on info query 6
bugs.description: AI called create_itinerary_items on message 6 ('Recommend something fun to do tonight.'). The server-side guardrail blocked the call and returned status=not_needed, so no items were actually created. This is the exact trigger that caused issues in Run 5 N-01 msg5. The prompt still does not distinguish 'recommend' from 'add' reliably. Backend safety net caught it.
bugs.expected: No toolCall for create_itinerary_items on an informational recommendation query
bugs.actual: toolCall with toolName=create_itinerary_items was emitted in the stream, blocked by server-side guardrail

ux_issues.description: Messages 2 and 6 produced duplicate response content. After the server-side guardrail rejected the create_itinerary_items tool call, the AI repeated its entire answer (recommending the same restaurant/Fado venue twice in one message). This looks broken to the user.
ux_issues.suggestion: When the tool result indicates not_needed, the AI should seamlessly continue rather than re-stating its recommendation. This may require tuning the tool rejection message or adding post-processing to deduplicate.

ux_issues.description: Message 2 included 'My apologies, I misspoke. I should not have added that to your itinerary directly without you asking.' — this is confusing because nothing was actually added. The AI's self-correction text implies a mutation happened when it didn't.
ux_issues.suggestion: The AI should not apologize for an action that was blocked. The guardrail message should instruct the AI to continue naturally without acknowledging the failed tool call.

ai_behavior_issues.issue: AI attempted to call create_itinerary_items on 2 out of 6 purely informational companion-mode queries (messages 2 and 6). Prompt discipline is insufficient — the AI still conflates 'recommend' and 'what's a good X' with 'add to my plan'.
ai_behavior_issues.context: Messages 2 and 6 triggered toolCall events. Server-side guardrail blocked both. Messages 1, 3, 4, 5 correctly avoided the tool.

usefulness_evaluation.narrative: The companion mode provides genuinely useful, concise answers to informational queries about Lisbon. The AI correctly answered all 6 questions with helpful local knowledge. However, on 2 of 6 messages the AI attempted to call create_itinerary_items (blocked by server-side guardrail), and the resulting duplicate response text and false apology degrade the UX. The itinerary was never mutated (N_BEFORE=12, N_AFTER=12), so the backend safety net works, but the prompt needs further tuning to prevent unnecessary tool calls on info queries.
